test.name: Empty folder with no research.json should route to init-project
test.description: The user opens an empty folder (no research.json, no tree.gedcomx.json) and asks 'where are we?'. project-status reports on EXISTING research; with no project to summarize, the correct routing is init-project to start one. SKILL.md description: 'Do NOT use when no research.json exists in the folder (use init-project instead).'

input.user_message: Where are we on this research?

negative.explanation: There is no research.json in the folder, so there is no project state to summarize. project-status is the 'resume an existing project' skill; with nothing to resume, the user needs to create a project first. init-project owns the initial creation of research.json + tree.gedcomx.json. Producing an empty or fabricated status report instead of routing to init-project would be wrong.

judge_context: Should recognize that no research.json exists and route to init-project rather than producing a status report
Should NOT fabricate a project summary or invent project state that isn't there
